name: Can ChatGPT really give me legal advice for my New York rental dispute or is it dangerous?
description: No, it can't give reliable advice; verify with a real NY lawyer to avoid errors.[1]

mainEntity.name: Can ChatGPT really give me legal advice for my New York rental dispute or is it dangerous?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, it can't give reliable advice; verify with a real NY lawyer to avoid errors.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: ChatGPT cannot provide accurate legal advice and may hallucinate facts or laws, making it dangerous for real cases.[1] It's useful for overviews or brainstorming but requires lawyer verification, especially in jurisdiction-specific matters like New York contracts.[1] Key factors: Lacks judgment and ethical duties; privacy risks with client data; past cases show fabricated citations.[2] Use it only for initial research, then see a New York-barred attorney; never file AI outputs directly. Lawyers remain essential.
